title: Windows Forensics: Endpoint Phishing Chain — Live PowerShell
description: WS-FIN-04 ran a macro from a phishing email this morning. Walk the live Sysmon stream, scheduled tasks, and TCP connection state to reconstruct macro → encoded PowerShell → dropped binary → C2.

hints.text: `connect ws-fin-04`. Start with `Get-WinEvent | include WINWORD` to land on the first event in the chain.
`Get-WinEvent | include EncodedCommand` exposes the base64 blob. Decode it with `printf '<blob>' | base64 -d` (UTF-8 base64 for this exercise).
`Get-NetTCPConnection | include Established` cross-references with `Get-Process` to tell you which PID owns the C2 socket.
